Title: Happy Path — Single Node Full Upgrade
Description: Run the full pipeline against a single known node and verify all stages complete without error.
Expected_Results: Precheck passes with knife --version in logs | reports/raw/tagged_nodes.json created with status=tagged | tag_success.list contains testnode01 | prepend_role.json created with role prepended | knife node show confirms role[chef_upgrade_cron] is first in run list | Pipeline result: SUCCESS

Title: Happy Path — Multiple Nodes in Parallel
Description: Run the full pipeline against 5+ nodes with MAX_PARALLEL set to 3.
Inputs: NODE_LIST=5 valid node names (one per line) | UPGRADE_TAG=upgrade19 | MAX_PARALLEL=3 | DRY_RUN=false
Expected_Results: All 5 nodes in tagged_nodes.json and tag_success.list | prepend_role.json shows all 5 nodes with role prepended | Logs show at most 3 concurrent knife invocations | Pipeline result: SUCCESS

Title: Dry Run — No Live Mutations
Description: Verify that when DRY_RUN=true no changes are made on the Chef server.
Pre_Conditions: Record current tags and run list of the node before the run.
Expected_Results: All knife mutation commands printed but not executed (prefixed [DRY RUN]) | tagged_nodes.json and prepend_role.json generated with dry-run status | knife node show confirms tags and run list unchanged after run | Pipeline result: SUCCESS

Title: Empty NODE_LIST Fails Fast
Description: Submit the pipeline with no node names and confirm it fails at Precheck before touching the Chef server.
Expected_Results: Precheck fails with 'NODE_LIST parameter is empty' error | Stages Tag Nodes and Prepend Bootstrap Role never entered | No reports/raw/*.json files created | Pipeline result: FAILURE

Title: Non-Existent Node Handling
Description: Include one node name that does not exist on the Chef server and verify the pipeline handles it gracefully.
Expected_Results: tagged_nodes.json records ghost-node-99999 with status=failed | tag_success.list does not contain ghost-node-99999 | prepend_role.json only includes the valid node | Pipeline exits non-zero (FAILURE or UNSTABLE) | Valid node tagged and role prepended correctly

Title: Idempotency — Re-run on Already-Tagged Nodes
Description: Run the pipeline twice in succession against the same node list and confirm the second run does not error.
Inputs: Same NODE_LIST / UPGRADE_TAG / BOOTSTRAP_ROLE as TC-01
Expected_Results: Second run completes without error | tagged_nodes.json shows nodes as tagged (no duplicate tag applied) | prepend_role.json shows role present (run list not duplicated) | Pipeline result: SUCCESS

Title: Remove Role — Cleanup After Upgrade Completes
Description: After a successful upgrade nodes report target version; run remove_role.sh and verify bootstrap role is removed.
Pre_Conditions: Nodes already tagged with role[chef_upgrade_cron] prepended (TC-01 or TC-02 completed).
Expected_Results: Nodes on chef-client 19.* have role[chef_upgrade_cron] removed from run list | Nodes on older version retain the role | Log entries confirm per-node action (removed vs skipped) | Script exits 0

Title: Remove Role — Dry Run
Description: Run remove_role.sh with --dry-run and verify no actual changes are made.
Expected_Results: All intended actions printed to stdout (e.g. [DRY RUN] knife node run_list remove ...) | knife node show confirms run lists unchanged after run | Script exits 0

Title: Chef Credentials Override
Description: Verify pipeline honours CHEF_SERVER_URL / CHEF_CLIENT_NAME / CHEF_CLIENT_KEY when provided instead of ~/.chef/credentials.
Inputs: NODE_LIST=one valid node | CHEF_SERVER_URL=valid test URL | CHEF_CLIENT_NAME=valid client name | CHEF_CLIENT_KEY=valid key path on agent | DRY_RUN=false
Expected_Results: All knife calls use supplied credentials (--server-url flag visible in logs) | Pipeline completes without reading ~/.chef/credentials | Pipeline result: SUCCESS

Title: Concurrent Build Lock
Description: Trigger two simultaneous builds with the same NODE_LIST and verify disableConcurrentBuilds() prevents a race condition.
Inputs: Same params submitted twice in quick succession
Expected_Results: Second build waits in queue until first completes | Both builds complete without duplicate tag or run-list entries on any node | Jenkins build history shows sequential (non-overlapping) execution times
